title: Wireless Design, Configuration, and Validation · PBQ Checkpoint
summary: Design staff, guest, and point-to-point wireless service across crowded, regulated, and mobile spaces. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a secure wireless survey and deployment plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare relevant 802.11 capabilities across 2.4, 5, and 6 GHz bands, channel availability, range, and client support.
Choose channel, width, reuse, and band steering from regulatory domain, overlap, contention, capacity, and coverage.
Distinguish SSID, BSSID, ESS, hidden names, and client discovery without treating an SSID as a security control.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.
